title: Modules, Packages, and Dependency Boundaries
description: Split the status coordinator into explicit browser modules and verify clean-environment loading and dependency behavior. Learners cumulatively produce a modular status application with a dependency record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use named, default, namespace, side-effect, and re-export forms with explicit public boundaries.
Trace static module graph linking, live bindings, execution order, and cyclic dependency risks.
Use dynamic import for conditional capability or measured loading needs with complete error states.
